_meta.source: Empirical testing via vamos against 1270 instruction variants

cli.usage: GenAm FROM/A TO/K WITH/K INCDIR/K/M HEADER/K/M QUIET/S
cli.notes: GenAm always wraps output in Amiga hunk format; no -Fbin equivalent

options.notes: Each OPT must be on its own line; comma-separated OPTs on one line cause errors

auto_config.behavior: Auto-loaded from GenAm's directory if present
auto_config.vamos_issue: incdir paths in shipped opts file don't exist under vamos, causing errors

org.description: Sets program counter to absolute value; generates position-dependent code
org.restriction: Not allowed for linkable code (default Amiga executable mode)
org.notes: Normal AmigaDOS programs do not need ORG. More than one ORG allowed but no padding is done.

pc_relative_star.description: Current PC reference; works in all output modes including linkable code
pc_relative_star.notes: Used for branch targets in testing since ORG is forbidden in default executable mode

encoding_behaviors.description: Routes ADD/AND/OR/SUB/EOR #imm,Dn to dedicated immediate instruction (ADDI/ANDI/ORI/SUBI/EORI), but NOT CMP→CMPI
encoding_behaviors.behavior: When assembling <OP> #imm,Dn, GenAm emits the ADDI/ANDI/etc. encoding rather than the general-purpose <OP> with immediate EA mode. Exception: CMP #imm,Dn keeps the general CMP encoding (does not route to CMPI)
encoding_behaviors.note: Both encodings produce identical results; ADDI is shorter for .l size (4 bytes vs 6 bytes for ADD #imm,Dn)

encoding_behaviors.description: EXG may swap Rx/Ry register assignment
encoding_behaviors.behavior: For same-type exchanges (Ax,Ay or Dx,Dy), GenAm may assign operands to Rx/Ry in reversed order compared to syntactic order
encoding_behaviors.note: EXG is commutative; swapping Rx/Ry produces identical behavior
